authoritative_sources.title: Mortgage Servicing Resources
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. consumer-finance regulator for mortgage servicing, and this resource hub is its official baseline for servicing rules, implementation support, and borrower-impact controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: lending-servicing rule selection and borrower-impact control review
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains public-baseline workflow claims and keeps the lane from improvising borrower-impact guidance beyond official servicing rules.

authoritative_sources.title: 12 CFR 1024.38 General Servicing Policies, Procedures, and Requirements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This is authoritative Regulation X rule text governing servicing policies, information management, transfer handling, and service-provider oversight.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: intake controls, servicing transfer readiness, and record-management validation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines what evidence and system readiness must exist before the lane treats a loan as safely boardable, transferable, or processable.

authoritative_sources.title: Official Interpretation to 12 CFR 1026.36 - Prompt Crediting of Payments and Payoff Statements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This is the authoritative CFPB interpretation of TILA servicing requirements for payment crediting and payoff statement handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment posting, payoff support, and borrower communication review
used_for: refusal of unsupported backdating or closure
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains payment-posting and payoff scenarios so the lane cites evidence and escalates conflicts instead of inventing compliant treatment.

authoritative_sources.title: Servicer Responsibilities for Processing Mortgage Loan Payments
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Fannie Mae publishes the controlling public servicing guide for loans serviced to its standards, making this a direct workflow source for payment processing, suspense handling, and remittance expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment application, suspense review, and remittance handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supplies realistic loan-servicing terminology and workflow states so the package does not collapse into generic payments processing language.

authoritative_sources.title: Post-Delivery Servicing Transfers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Fannie Mae's servicing-transfer guide is a public operating rule for transfer timing, data accuracy, and handoff responsibilities after delivery.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains boarding and transfer scenarios so the lane blocks incomplete handoffs and names missing transfer evidence instead of treating local trackers as authoritative.

authoritative_sources.title: FFIEC BSA/AML Manual - Lending Activities
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FFIEC provides the interagency examination guidance used by U.S. banking regulators for AML and suspicious-activity risk in lending activities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: suspicious payment, payoff, or borrower-behavior escalation
used_for: boundary against fraud or AML disposition
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines when loan-operations work must stop and route to fraud or AML specialists instead of narrating suspicious behavior away as routine servicing.

authoritative_sources.title: Servicing Transfer Catalog
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: MISMO is the recognized mortgage data standards body, and the Servicing Transfer Catalog is the vendor-neutral standard package for timely and accurate loan-information transfer between servicers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: data completeness review during servicing transfer and post-transfer exception handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supplies evidence and data-quality expectations for transfer packets and allows the pack to define missing-data failure modes precisely.

authoritative_sources.title: Loan Boarding Data Segment of the Industry Transfer of Servicing Dataset
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: MISMO's loan boarding data segment is a public, vendor-neutral standard for the data elements and handoff completeness required to board transferred loans accurately.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: boarding evidence review and record-of-truth setup
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines the minimum record set needed before the lane can treat loan boarding as safe enough to advance.

authoritative_sources.title: Risk Management Topics - October 1, 2024
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Nacha is the ACH network rules body, and its public risk-management topics define control expectations for unauthorized debits, returns, and exception handling on recurring payment flows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ACH loan-payment exception review and return handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains ACH-return and unapplied-funds scenarios so the lane records evidence and routes correctly instead of improvising settlement or remediation treatment.

workflow_stages.name: intake and prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm borrower, loan, product, investor, jurisdiction, approval, and source-of-record prerequisites before a lending queue item progresses.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: boarding, payment, and payoff execution handling
workflow_stages.description: Advance, hold, or rework boarding, payment application, suspense, escrow, and payoff items based on authoritative servicing records and visible approvals.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: servicing-transfer and record-of-truth reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Validate loan-transfer completeness, boarding data, collateral and document alignment, and system-of-record setup before treating transfer work as complete.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: exception queue and borrower-impact conflict handling
workflow_stages.description: Use payment returns, suspense logs, servicing notes, complaint records, and policy references to determine whether items stay open, rework, or escalate.

workflow_stages.name: fraud, aml, and consumer-protection escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop work and route to the correct specialist when suspicious activity, borrower-harm risk, or legal-interpretation conflicts change the outcome.

artifacts: loan boarding checklist, investor transfer packet, and approval-history artifacts
promissory note, collateral receipt, lien or MERS data, and trailing-document records
loan payment history, suspense ledger, unapplied-funds log, and delinquency state records
ACH, wire, or lockbox payment references including return or reversal codes
escrow analysis records, payoff quote worksheets, payoff funds confirmations, and zero-balance evidence
servicing notes, borrower complaints, disclosures, notices, and communication logs
fraud-monitoring, AML, bankruptcy, SCRA, and exception-case artifacts
general-ledger tie-outs, investor remittance records, and reconciliation exceptions
systems_of_record: payment hub, ACH or wire processing system, and lockbox platform
fraud monitoring, AML case-management, and legal-compliance escalation systems

allowed: Determine whether available records support progression, hold, rework, or completion within bounded lending-servicing workflows.
Produce execution summaries, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packets tied to named loan, payment, payoff, transfer, and communication records.
Request the minimum tenant facts, investor rules, and jurisdiction context needed to continue safely.
escalate: A fraud, AML, suspicious-payment, or borrower-impact indicator changes the risk posture.
A legal, disclosure, bankruptcy, SCRA, payoff, or policy interpretation materially changes whether the request may proceed.
Loan-servicing, payment, transfer, or reconciliation records conflict and need human review or adjacent-owner resolution.
refuse: The request asks for underwriting, credit, workout, collections, lien-release approval, legal interpretation, or final fraud or AML disposition outside the lane.
The request asks to waive fees, backdate payment crediting, or mark work complete without authoritative evidence.
Required tenant facts, investor rules, product classification, jurisdiction context, or system-of-record evidence are missing and would change the outcome.

domain_failure_modes: Advancing loan boarding without complete transfer, collateral, or boarding evidence
Crediting payments, applying payoff funds, or releasing liens when authoritative servicing and ledger records do not align
Treating suspense, unapplied-funds, and ACH return states as resolved without supported rework evidence
Failing to escalate suspicious payoffs, third-party payments, or AML-sensitive borrower behavior
Missing borrower-impact implications when transfer notices, payment posting dates, or payoff communications conflict
Generalizing investor or product rules across programs without tenant evidence
Collapsing operational handling into underwriting, loss-mitigation, collections strategy, or legal interpretation

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Normal in-scope execution with realistic terminology
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Non-mortgage lending lines and investor-specific operating rules
unresolved_ambiguities.details: The public baseline is strongest for U.S. mortgage servicing and ACH-backed loan-payment workflows. Tenant retrieval is required when auto, student, HELOC, commercial, or private-investor servicing rules materially change boarding, payoff, or borrower-impact handling.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-law foreclosure, bankruptcy, SCRA, and lien-release variation
unresolved_ambiguities.details: Jurisdiction-specific legal rules can change required notices, timing, and permissible actions. The lane remains non-legal, refuses interpretation, and requires tenant or legal retrieval before such variation affects the workflow outcome.
